content.problemStatement:
Chef owns a fast food shop that is currently located in a city called Chefland. He is planning to move his shop to a bigger city called Chefabad. There are NNN consecutive days (numbered 111 through NNN) such that the move can be done before day 111, after day NNN or between any two consecutive days. Moving the shop takes zero time. Chef wants to time the move in a way thay maximises his profit during these NNN days.

For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), we know that if Chef's shop is in Chefland on the iii-th day, he earns AiA_iAi​ coins, while if it is in Chefabad on this day, he earns BiB_iBi​ coins. Find the maximum profit Chef can make if he optimally chooses the time when the shop should move. Note that once the shop has moved to Chefabad, it cannot return to Chefland.
constraints: 1≤Ai,Bi≤1041 \le A_i, B_i \le 10^41≤Ai​,Bi​≤104 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum profit.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The best time to move is before the first day.
Example case 2: The best time to move is after the last day.
Example case 3: The best time to move is after the first day.
